summary: This cluster focuses on the discovery and taxonomic characterization of new green algal species from the mesophotic and bloom-prone coastal waters of Jeju Island, Korea. Researchers employed DNA barcoding of the *rbcL* and *tufA* genes to identify five bloom-forming *Ulva* species, including the novel ecotype *U. pseudo-ohnoi* sp. nov., which contributes to the frequent green tides in the Yellow Sea. Concurrently, a distinct iridescent species, *Umbraulva yunseulla* sp. nov., was described from subtidal habitats, possessing the pigment siphonaxanthin that enables its deep-water survival. Together, these studies underscore the high, previously unrecognized macroalgal biodiversity in Korean mesophotic ecosystems and the necessity of molecular phylogenetics for distinguishing cryptic bloom-forming taxa.
